speaker: Davicho Barona
text: I… I… it's a good question, because I honestly… at first, I used to always just be really heavy on the description, and I was like, if I describe it the same way every time, then the agent should obviously understand that every time… or the model should understand that every time I'm doing this, it should be the same… the same result, or the same style, or whatever.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: But over time, I've realized that that's not actually the case, because what happens is.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: every time when you're describing it, which is basically a prompt, you are asking the agent or the model to go back and look at its training data. And so a lot of times, it's pulling out something slightly different. You know, like, let's say, for example, if we

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: if I'm working with a brand, like a car brand, a Toyota or something, and I have these perfect Toyota references, and that they sent me. They sent me every car, every angle.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: I now have this choice. Do I, every time I make something, do I describe, or do I give it the visual reference that I have?

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: And the answer is, if you have a perfect visual reference, always use the visual reference without the prompt, because if I go in there and I start describing the car, the red Toyota with whatever wheels and the year, and all the different things.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: that…

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: are that reference, but I'm describing it via prompt and via text, I'm now telling the agent, like, yes, look at the car that I'm giving this reference, but also go and dig through your training data and find a red Toyota with the… and so all of a sudden, you're…

speaker: Elle Cardenas
text: So…

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: Visual references, 100% preferred over prompt and description, because if you have a visual reference, that's gonna be the number one way of keeping the agent, or the model.

speaker: Davicho Barona
text: True to the… to the thing you're trying to make, is giving those, those visual references.
